title: Cambridge C1 Advanced — Listening
instructions: You will hear a number of different recordings and you have to answer questions on what you hear. There are four parts to the test. You will hear each extract twice.

parts.title: Part 1 — Multiple choice
parts.instructions: You will hear three different extracts. For questions 1–6, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best according to what you hear. There are two questions for each extract.

passage.content:
Extract One
You hear part of a discussion between two marine biologists about coral reefs.

Extract Two
You hear two friends talking about birdwatching.

Extract Three
You hear a choreographer and a dancer talking about their work.

audio.note: Full CAE listening test audio — covers all four parts. You will hear each extract twice within the recording.

questions.prompt: 1. How does the woman feel about current research on coral reefs?

options.text: afraid that its value may be underestimated
worried that not enough time is being spent on it
concerned about shortcomings in what the data is measuring

questions.prompt: 2. When talking about conserving coral reefs, the man

options.text: outlines decisions that have affected it.
suggests practical approaches to take to it.
identifies specific projects that have been successful.

questions.prompt: 3. Why has the woman's opinion of birdwatching changed?

options.text: She experienced the excitement of recognising an unusual species.
She was persuaded by the man's enthusiasm for the activity.
She had the opportunity to witness natural behaviour first hand.

questions.prompt: 4. The man says that while birdwatching, he has developed

options.text: an interest in promoting the conservation of certain creatures.
an appreciation of the astonishing variety of species that exist.
an understanding of the connection between an environment and its wildlife.

questions.prompt: 5. What does the man think about his work as a choreographer?

options.text: There is constant pressure on him to produce popular ballets.
It's important for him to seek out new sources of inspiration.
He has a responsibility to widen people's experiences.

questions.prompt: 6. What do they both think about dancers and choreographers creating new ballets together?

options.text: It's a physically demanding way of working.
It can be difficult to achieve genuine collaboration.
It may not always produce a satisfactory outcome.

parts.title: Part 2 — Sentence completion
parts.instructions: You will hear an archaeology student called Hannah Miller giving a talk to a group of fellow students about her first archaeological excavation, known as a dig. For questions 7–14, complete the sentences with a word or short phrase.

passage.content:
FIRST-YEAR ARCHAEOLOGICAL DIG

Complete each sentence with a word or short phrase from what Hannah says.

audio.note: Part 2 is in the second section of the shared CAE listening audio.

questions.prompt: 7. Hannah warns that there is more __________ involved in a dig than most students expect.
8. Hannah struggled with distinguishing between the __________ that the team found as they dug.
9. Hannah says that past __________ sometimes made it difficult to determine which materials belonged to which building.
10. Hannah thinks that __________ are the type of object that is hard to identify if you lack experience.
11. Hannah explains why archaeologists need to be careful about using __________ while a dig is ongoing.
12. Hannah feels that __________ is a good way to combat theft and vandalism around archaeological digs.
13. Hannah was particularly grateful for the support that the __________ gave her during the dig.
14. Doing her first dig helped Hannah realise that __________ was the aspect of archaeology she'd like to study in the future.

parts.title: Part 3 — Multiple choice (long interview)
parts.instructions: You will hear an interview with two chefs, Pearl Benuga and Jim Davis. For questions 15–20, choose the answer (A, B, C or D) which fits best according to what you hear.

audio.note: Part 3 is in the third section of the shared CAE listening audio.

questions.prompt: 15. What does Pearl say about working in her father's restaurant?

options.text: She wasn't given special treatment.
She didn't appreciate her good fortune.
She didn't accomplish as much as expected.
She wasn't confident about her level of experience.

questions.prompt: 16. What aspect of Jim's job gives him the most satisfaction?

options.text: the knowledge that his work is appreciated
the close relationships he has built up
the security of having a regular income
the praise he receives from colleagues

questions.prompt: 17. What constant aspect of her work does Pearl dislike?

options.text: having to find new staff
having to check for imperfections
having to give instructions
having to stick to a schedule

questions.prompt: 18. What do Pearl and Jim say inspires them?

options.text: exposure to the ideas of other chefs
discussions about food with other people
unconventional recipes they come across online
limited availability of ingredients they can use

questions.prompt: 19. When Jim goes to other people's restaurants, he usually

options.text: assesses whether they're a threat to his business.
tries to ensure no-one recognises him.
feels annoyed at the unreasonable prices.
selects dishes that require skill to produce.

questions.prompt: 20. What does Pearl say about her future plans?

options.text: They will be quite hard to finance.
They will involve big changes.
They reflect her earlier interests.
They are not very well developed.

parts.title: Part 4 — Multiple matching (two tasks)
parts.instructions: You will hear five short extracts in which people are talking about their work making and restoring violins. Look at Task 1 and Task 2. For each question, choose the answer (A, B, C, D, E, F or G). Complete both tasks as you listen.

audio.note: Part 4 is in the final section of the shared CAE listening audio.

taskGroups.instructions: Task 1 — For questions 21–25, choose from the list (A–G) what each speaker says inspired them to learn to make and restore violins.

options.text: having the chance to run a business
following the recommendation of a friend
playing someone else's violin
handling the natural materials used
observing an expert at work
discovering the challenges of the job
hearing someone talk about their passion

questions.prompt: Speaker 1
Speaker 2
Speaker 3
Speaker 4
Speaker 5

taskGroups.instructions: Task 2 — For questions 26–30, choose from the list (A–G) what each speaker says about their work now.

options.text: Having to deal with paperwork is distracting.
It's disappointing when people underestimate what's involved.
It's often thrilling to identify the original maker.
The craft has taken a long time to master.
Working on antiques is a privilege.
Earning a living is far from straightforward.
There's a huge demand for their work.

questions.prompt: Speaker 1
Speaker 2
Speaker 3
Speaker 4
Speaker 5
